qa_pairs.question: Why do Digital Research Alliance of Canada (Alliance) accounts require annual renewal, and what are the consequences if an account is not renewed by the deadline?
qa_pairs.answer: Alliance accounts require annual renewal to collect up-to-date information for granting agencies and to deactivate accounts that are no longer in use. All users must renew their accounts between May 6 and June 9, 2026. If an account is not renewed by this date, it will be automatically deactivated, preventing access to data, although the data will not be immediately deleted. Deactivated accounts can be renewed at any time.

qa_pairs.question: What is a primary role in the Digital Research Alliance of Canada (Alliance) account system, and why is it important to renew it annually?
qa_pairs.answer: A primary role corresponds to a user's main position, such as a full-time faculty appointment or the role associated with their primary supervisor. It is important because it helps manage accounts with multiple roles, facilitates communication with users, and aids in reporting. If a primary role is not renewed during the annual renewal period, the user's entire Alliance account will be deactivated.

qa_pairs.question: What are the specific consequences for a faculty member and their sponsored users if the faculty member does not renew their Digital Research Alliance of Canada (Alliance) account by the deadline?
qa_pairs.answer: If a faculty member does not renew their Alliance account between May 6 and June 9, 2026, their account will be automatically deactivated. Furthermore, the accounts of all users sponsored by that faculty member will also be deactivated. This will result in all jobs submitted by the faculty member or their sponsored users being paused, and the research group will lose access to their files until the sponsor's account is reactivated.
